name: Current Affairs in Indian Polity (6): The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026

sourceRefs.label: prsindia.org/pib.gov.in - Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026 (introduced 27 March 2026, passed both Houses)

researchNotes: Sixth Polity current-affairs topic (6th lap). A successfully PASSED bill — useful contrast with the Day-75 topic's 131st Amendment Bill, which was REJECTED — together illustrating both possible outcomes of the legislative process for students. Expansion pass (Batch 19): added the original 2023 Jan Vishwas Act's scale for comparison, showing this is an ongoing reform strategy.

reading.intro: Covers the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026 — a major decriminalisation reform passed by Parliament in 2026.

qa.question: When was the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026 introduced, and what is its current status?
qa.answer: Introduced in the Lok Sabha on 27 March 2026; it has since been PASSED by both Houses of Parliament.

qa.question: What is the Bill's core purpose?
qa.answer: To decriminalise or rationalise offences and penalties across central Acts — promoting 'ease of doing business' and 'ease of living' by removing/softening minor criminal penalties for technical or procedural lapses.

qa.question: How many Central Acts and Ministries does the Bill cover, and how many total provisions does it amend?
qa.answer: 79 Central Acts, administered by 23 Ministries, covering 784 total provisions.

qa.question: How are the 784 provisions split between decriminalisation and ease-of-living amendments?
qa.answer: 717 provisions are decriminalised (Ease of Doing Business); 67 provisions are amended for Ease of Living.

qa.question: What was the FIRST Jan Vishwas Act, and how does the 2026 Bill relate to it?
qa.answer: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2023 was the original decriminalisation reform, amending 42 Acts across 19 Ministries. The 2026 Bill is a SECOND, larger round of the same reform philosophy — extending decriminalisation to a much larger set of 79 Acts and 23 Ministries, showing this is now an ongoing legislative strategy rather than a one-time exercise.

questions.prompt: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026 was introduced in the Lok Sabha on:
The Jan Vishwas Bill, 2026's core purpose is to:
options: Increase criminal penalties across all laws
Decriminalise/rationalise offences and penalties across central Acts

questions.prompt: The Jan Vishwas Bill, 2026 amends how many Central Acts?
Of the Bill's 784 total provisions, how many are decriminalised for Ease of Doing Business?
Unlike the Day-75-covered 131st Constitutional Amendment Bill (which was rejected), the Jan Vishwas Bill, 2026 was:
options: Passed by both Houses of Parliament
Referred indefinitely to a Select Committee
questions.explanation: Passed by both Houses of Parliament.

questions.prompt: The original Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2023 amended how many Acts across how many Ministries?
